summary: Large mncount decompilation pass for the Data / Melee Records / Misc Records menu. The PR replaced many placeholder or address-based mnCount functions with typed implementations, row enums, menu userdata fields, SIS text creation, scrolling/input handling, arrow indicator updates, and named stat getter helpers. It also propagated better names for related gmMainLib stats getters, gm play-time/match-total helpers, power count/time helpers, and un_GetTrophyTotal across callers. The decomp.dev bot reported +1724 matched code bytes and +16 matched data bytes, with new full matches for mnCount_HandleUserInput, mnCount_8025072C, mncount .bss, and mnCount_8025035C, plus near-complete improvements for several remaining mncount functions.
what_changed: Implemented most of src/melee/mn/mncount.c, expanding it from small placeholder-style functions into typed menu logic for the records-count screen.
Added mnCount_row enum covering visible record rows such as POWER_COUNT, PLAY_TIME, VS_PLAY_MATCH_TOTAL, TROPHY_TOTAL, LONGEST_TIME, SMASH_CHAMP, KO_KINGPIN, NO_DEFENSE_NELLY, and DISASTER_MASTER.
Reworked MnCountData from cursor/page_size fields into scroll_pos, expand_anim_duration, labels[10], values[10], and title, with MNCOUNT_VISIBLE_ROWS set to 10.

Added row value helpers: mnCount_GetRowValue_Character, mnCount_GetRowValue_Number, mnCount_CreateRow, mnCount_HandleUserInput, mnCount_UpdateArrowIndicators, mnCount_InitUserData, and mnCount_Create.
Replaced local placeholder fighter-data offsets with named persistent fighter data fields such as play_time, sd_count, victories, losses, damage_dealt, and damage_taken.

Added getter wrappers for character-stat rows: mnCount_GetMatchTime, mnCount_GetKOKingpin, mnCount_GetNoDefenseNelly, mnCount_GetDisasterMaster, mnCount_GetSmashChamp, mnCount_GetSmashSap, mnCount_GetSlugMeister, and mnCount_GetPunchingBag.
Implemented rank/tie-resolution helpers around CountEntry arrays for record leader rows, including mnCount_8025035C, mnCount_8025072C, and mnCount_8025092C.
Created SIS text rows with HSD_SisLib_803A5ACC/HSD_SisLib_803A6754, row label IDs from mnCount_sis_idx, formatted time rows, numeric rows, and character-name rows.

Renamed address-style gmMainLib stat accessors to semantic names like gmMainLib_GetVsPlayTime, gmMainLib_GetTimeMatchTotal, gmMainLib_GetPowerCount, gmMainLib_GetTotalDamage, gmMainLib_GetKOTotal, and gmMainLib_GetSelfDestructTotal.
Renamed gm_8016279C to gm_GetVsPlayMatchTotal, gm_80162A00 to gm_GetPlayTime, gm_80162B98 to gm_IncrementPowerCount, and un_80304870 to un_GetTrophyTotal, updating cross-file callers.
Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt to reflect the new semantic symbol names for gmMainLib, gm, mnCount, and trophy functions.
smart_moves: Introduced mnCount_row to make a table-like records menu understandable, replacing magic row numbers with meaningful row categories.
Centralized numeric row computation in mnCount_GetRowValue_Number and character row selection in mnCount_GetRowValue_Character, which makes menu rendering independent from stat lookup details.
Used small semantic getter functions for persistent fighter stats, letting generic ranking code accept function pointers without hard-coding each record type.
Preserved matching-sensitive behavior with explicit inline helpers and noinline wrappers, including mnCount_UpdateArrowIndicators_noinline and mnCount_InitUserData_noinline.
Kept GET_MNCOUNT conditional for M2C, suggesting compatibility with both normal typed GObj userdata access and m2c-generated casts.
Matched UI behavior by freeing/recreating HSD_Text objects for labels and values when rows update, rather than mutating all existing row text in place.
Applied saturation/clamping logic for displayable numeric values, with separate limits for time rows and non-time rows.
Propagated semantic renames across gm, ty, if, lb, and mn callers in the same PR so the symbol table, headers, and call sites stayed consistent.
decomp_lessons: For menu-record screens, first identify the row taxonomy and encode it as an enum; it makes rendering, input, and stat lookup code much easier to match and review.
Replacing anonymous local offset structs with existing persistent-data structs can unlock both readability and matching, as seen with GetPersistentFighterData fields.
Generic ranking helpers can be matched cleanly with function pointers when each stat has a tiny named getter.
When decompiling HSD/SIS UI code, track object lifetime carefully: this PR frees labels/values/title text before recreating or leaving the screen.
Cross-module stat accessors should be renamed as soon as their target fields are understood; downstream menu and achievement code becomes self-documenting.
Semantic renames should be synchronized across symbols.txt, C definitions, headers, and all call sites to avoid stale address-style names.
Matching menu code may require intentional inlining/noinlining structure, not just logically equivalent C.
Documenting constants such as NUM_CHARACTERS, NUM_STAGES, MAX_SCROLL, and MNCOUNT_VISIBLE_ROWS reduces magic numbers and helps preserve array sizes.
naming_conventions: mnCount_GetX is used for stat getter helpers, for example mnCount_GetMatchTime, mnCount_GetSmashChamp, and mnCount_GetPunchingBag.
mnCount_GetRowValue_Character and mnCount_GetRowValue_Number distinguish rows that display a character result from rows that display a numeric result.
gmMainLib_GetX names are used for accessors that return pointers into gmMainLib persistent stats, such as gmMainLib_GetVsPlayContestants and gmMainLib_GetSingleplayerTime.
gm_GetX or gm_IncrementX names are used for higher-level game-mode aggregate operations, such as gm_GetVsPlayMatchTotal, gm_GetPlayTime, and gm_IncrementPowerCount.
un_GetTrophyTotal replaced un_80304870, using a semantic getter name for trophy collection count.
mnCount_row enum values are all-caps row labels matching displayed record concepts, such as POWER_TIME, AVAILABLE_CHARACTERS, NO_DEFENSE_NELLY, and DISASTER_MASTER.
The macro GET_MNCOUNT(gobj) standardizes typed access to MnCountData userdata.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used static inline helpers for row classification and repeated UI operations: inline_is_row_time, inline_is_row_char, inline_update_entries, and inline_free_text.
Added explicit noinline wrapper functions around otherwise simple calls to influence codegen and matching: mnCount_UpdateArrowIndicators_noinline and mnCount_InitUserData_noinline.
Left matching-sensitive comments such as 'GET_JOBJ breaks this' near direct gobj->hsd_obj access in mnCount_UpdateArrowIndicators.
Kept stack-control artifacts such as PAD_STACK in nearby matched ranking code, indicating attention to stack layout.
Used macros GET_KOS and GET_FALLS locally inside mnCount_8025072C to express repeated stat lookups while preserving compact generated code.

Preserved HSD object/proc setup order in mnCount_Create and fn_80251640: load archive sections, create GObj, allocate userdata, initialize userdata, setup procs, create title text, then create input GObj.
Used explicit constants and unsigned clamping for time/numeric display bounds, likely reflecting original overflow/saturation behavior.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
decomp.dev bot reported matched code increased to 60.62% (+0.04%, +1724 bytes) and matched data to 37.72% (+16 bytes).
Bot-reported new full matches: mnCount_HandleUserInput, mnCount_8025072C, mncount .bss, and mnCount_8025035C.

Bot-reported near-complete improvements included mnCount_8025092C at 99.35%, mnCount_CreateRow at 96.51%, mnCount_GetRowValue_Number at 95.47%, fn_80251640 at 96.30%, mnCount_Create at 91.24%, and mnCount_GetRowValue_Character at 99.33%.
follow_up_queries: Check later PRs that finish the remaining near-matches in mnCount_Create, fn_802514D8, fn_80251640, mnCount_CreateRow, and mnCount_GetRowValue_Number.
Search for follow-up renames of remaining address-style mnCount functions fn_802514B8, fn_802514D8, fn_80251640, mnCount_8025035C, and mnCount_8025092C.
Look for subsequent cleanup of placeholder.h includes or replacement with narrower headers in mncount.c.
Search for further typing of gmMainLib_804D3EE0->thing fields around x1A18 through x1A60.
Compare later mncount matching work for additional noinline/inline tactics around HSD text creation and arrow indicator updates.
